<insights>

- Isolated files — no import edges between 13 files, cohesion is unmeasurable (reported as 0)
- Zone is composed entirely of static assets (docs, images, config, runtime state) with no importable source — correctly isolated from the main codebase graph
- The package uses its own tooling (rex) to track its own development work, making .rex/prd.json both a runtime artifact and a project management artifact
- README.md and context.md serve as human- and AI-readable entry points for understanding the package without running analysis
- Zero cohesion and zero coupling reflect that this zone is purely static files — the metric is expected and not a concern for this zone type.
- The presence of llms.txt and context.md alongside product assets confirms the package follows the n-dx pattern of self-describing via sourcevision output.
- No entry points means nothing imports these files at runtime, which is correct — these are human/AI-consumed documentation artifacts, not library modules.
- Rex-package-root and rex-e2e-fixtures both show zero coupling but serve orthogonal roles in the lifecycle: package-root contains product identity artifacts (README, images, runtime config) consumed by humans and AI tooling, while e2e-fixtures contains frozen runtime state consumed by automated tests. The distinction matters when deciding what changes each zone should absorb.
- The presence of both rex-package-root (documentation/identity) and rex-e2e-fixtures (test harness state) as separate zero-coupling zones confirms the monorepo correctly partitions all three content types — executable source, human documentation, and test infrastructure — into distinct communities with no cross-contamination.

</insights>
